data.name: BootSectorForm_AdditionalData
data.value:
This Is additional data found in the Boot Sector.
Note: Data highlighted in green is the bootstrap code.

data.name: BootSectorForm_AllowedValues
data.value: Allowed Values:

data.name: BootSectorForm_BootSectorSignature
data.value: Indicated to the BIOS that the sector is executable.

data.name: BootSectorForm_BytesPerSector
data.value: Number of bytes in each physical sector

data.name: BootSectorForm_DiskType
data.value: The disk type detected based on the current values in the Boot Record. Changing this will set the values in the boot record to those of the selected disk type.

data.name: BootSectorForm_DriveNumber
data.value: Interrupt 13h drive number

data.name: BootSectorForm_ExtendedBootSignature
data.value: 28h\tVolume Serial Number is present 29h\tVolume Serial Number, Volume Label, and File System ID are present

data.name: BootSectorForm_FileSystemType
data.value: The File System ID is informational only

data.name: BootSectorForm_FixedDisk
data.value: Fixed Disk

data.name: BootSectorForm_HiddenSectors
data.value:
Number of sectors preceeding the first sector of a partitioned volume
Note: This value should be {0} for all floppy disks

data.name: BootSectorForm_JumpInstruction
data.value: This instruction indicates where the bootstrap code starts.

data.name: BootSectorForm_NumberOfFATs
data.value:
Number of File Allocation Table (FAT) copies on the volume
Allowed Values: Any non-zero value
Note: This value should typically be set to {0}.

data.name: BootSectorForm_NumberOfHeads
data.value: Number of physical heads (sides) on the disk

data.name: BootSectorForm_OEMName
data.value:
OEM Name Identifier
Can be set by a FAT implementation to any desired value
Typically this is some indication of what system formatted the volume.

data.name: BootSectorForm_ReservedSectors
data.value:
Number of reserved sectors in the reserved region of the volume starting at the first sector of the volume
Allowed Values: Any non-zero value
Note: This value should typically be set to {0}.

data.name: BootSectorForm_RootDirectoryEntries
data.value:
Number of entries in the root directory
Allowed Values: Value multiplied by {0} should be an even multiple of Bytes Per Sector

data.name: BootSectorForm_SectorCountLarge
data.value:
Total number of sectors in a FAT16 volume larger than {0} sectors
Note: This value should be {1} for all floppy disks

data.name: BootSectorForm_SectorCountSmall
data.value: Total number of sectors in the volume

data.name: BootSectorForm_SectorsPerCluster
data.value: Number of sectors per cluster

data.name: BootSectorForm_SectorsPerFAT
data.value: Number of sectors allocated to each copy of the File Allocation Table (FAT)

data.name: BootSectorForm_SectorsPerTrack
data.value: Number of sectors per track on the disk

data.name: BootSectorForm_TypicalValues
data.value: Typical Values:

data.name: BootSectorForm_Unused
data.value: Unused

data.name: BootSectorForm_VolumeLabel
data.value: This field typically matches the 11-byte volume label in the root directory of the disk or has the value "{0}" if the volume label does not exist.

data.name: BootSectorForm_VolumeSerialNumber
data.value:
The Volume Serial Number is a 32-bit random number used in conjunction with the Volume Label for removable media tracking
Note: This id is typically generated by converting the current date and time into a 32-bit value

data.name: BootSectorForm_VolumeSerialNumberButton
data.value: Generate a new volume serial number based on a user supplied date and time

data.name: Greaseweazle_Revs
data.value: Number of revolutions to read per track

data.name: Greaseweazle_Retries
data.value: Number of retries per seek-retry

data.name: Greaseweazle_SeekRetries
data.value: Number of seek retries

data.name: Greaseweazle_DeviceReset
data.value: Reset the Greaseweazle device to power-on default state.

data.name: Flux_SaveLog
data.value: Save the Console Output to a log file.

data.name: Flux_Detect
data.value: Attempt to detect the format of the disk in the selected drive by analyzing the boot sector.

data.name: Greaseweazle_Drives
data.value:
Available Floppy Drives.
This list can be configured under Greaseweazle Configuration in the Options menu.

data.name: Flux_Format
data.value: The format of the floppy disk in the selected drive.

data.name: Flux_ImageType
data.value:
The output type for the read.
Flux Image Set saves the raw flux as a .raw set that can be re-converted later. The other types (.ima, .img, .hfe, ...) decode the disk directly into a sector image.

data.name: Greaseweazle_ReadFilename
data.value:
The output file’s name.
You can create a filename template by enclosing any number in angle brackets (< >). For example: Disk <1> of 3.
The number inside the brackets will automatically increment for each subsequent read.

data.name: Greaseweazle_FileExt
data.value: The output file's extension.

data.name: Greaseweazle_SaveLog
data.value:
Save a log file alongside the flux image set, capturing the Greaseweazle console output for the run.
The log file name can be configured under Greaseweazle Configuration in the Options menu.

data.name: Flux_Discard
data.value: Discard the currently loaded image or image set.

data.name: Flux_Read
data.value:
Read a floppy disk from the selected drive to the selected output type.
When Select Tracks is enabled, only the tracks selected in the grid below are re-read.

data.name: Greaseweazle_RootFolder
data.value:
The root folder for your flux image sets.
The default root folder can be configured under Greaseweazle Configuration in the Options menu.

data.name: Flux_DoubleStep
data.value: Skip every other track when reading a 360 KB disk in a 1.2 MB drive.

data.name: Flux_Import
data.value: Import the currently loaded image.

data.name: Flux_ImportClose
data.value: Import the currently loaded image and close this dialog.

data.name: Flux_Save
data.value: Save the currently loaded image set.

data.name: Flux_SaveClose
data.value: Save the currently loaded image set and close this dialog.

data.name: Flux_Convert
data.value: Convert the currently loaded flux image set into the selected image type without re-reading the drive.

data.name: Flux_Preview
data.value:
Preview the contents of the disk in the selected drive without saving an image, or preview the currently loaded image.
For an unread disk, the first track is read and the directory listing is displayed.

data.name: Flux_Refine
data.value:
Open an existing flux image set (.raw) and load its current state into the track grid.
The form is then placed in select-tracks mode so individual tracks can be re-read against the original flux set.

data.name: Flux_RootBrowse
data.value: Browse for the root folder where flux image sets are saved.

data.name: Flux_ToggleSequence
data.value:
Wrap or unwrap the current number with angle brackets (< >) in the focused filename, folder, or prefix field.
A bracketed number is auto-incremented after each read.

data.name: Flux_SelectTracks
data.value:
Enable per-track selection in the grid below.
When checked, only the tracks selected in the grid are re-read on the next Read.

data.name: Flux_FolderName
data.value:
The subfolder under the Root Folder where the flux image set is saved.
You can create a folder name template by enclosing any number in angle brackets (< >). For example: Disk <1> of 3.
The number inside the brackets will automatically increment for each subsequent read.

data.name: Flux_PrefixName
data.value:
Filename prefix for the .raw track files in the flux image set (for example, "track" produces track00.0.raw, track00.1.raw, ...).
You can create a prefix template by enclosing any number in angle brackets (< >). For example: side<1>.
The number inside the brackets will automatically increment for each subsequent read.

data.name: BootSectorForm_AllowedValuesInline
data.value: Allowed Values: {0}

data.name: BootSectorForm_TypicalValuesInline
data.value: Typical Values: {0}

data.name: BootSectorForm_BytesPerSector_Note
data.value: Note: This value should be {0} for all floppy disks.
